methods.description: Process for culturing the diatom Cylindrotheca closterium under control and Si-starvation conditions, and harvesting cells for further analysis.

steps.description: The diatom Cylindrotheca closterium was cultured in Guillard's F/2 medium. Experimental culturing was performed in triplicate for both control (CTRL) or Si-starvation conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: culture diatoms in F/2 medium under control and Si-starvation conditions

steps.description: Culturing was carried out in 2-litre polycarbonate bottles, constant bubbling with air filtered through 0.2 μm membrane filters in a climate chamber at 19 °C on a 12:12 hours light:dark cycle and at 100 μmol photons m−2 s−1.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain diatom cultures in climate chamber with specific conditions
inputs: air filtered through 0.2 μm membrane filters

steps.description: Initial cell concentrations were approximately 5000 cells/mL for each replicate, and culture growth was daily monitored from samples fixed with one drop of Lugol (final concentration of about 2%) and counted in a Bürker counting chamber under an Axioskop 2 microscope (20×) (Carl Zeiss GmbH).
steps.searchable_summary: monitor and count diatom cell growth daily

steps.description: Aliquots of 50 ml were sampled when the stationary phase was reached (Day 7), and centrifuged for 15 minutes at 4 °C at 1900 g (Eppendorf, 5810R).
steps.searchable_summary: sample and centrifuge diatom cultures at stationary phase

steps.description: For RNA extraction, microalgal pellets were resuspended in 500 μL of TRIZOL©, incubated for 2-3 min at 60 °C until completely dissolved, frozen in liquid nitrogen and kept at −80 °C till RNA extraction procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare diatom pellets for RNA extraction

steps.description: For chemical extraction and anti-inflammatory activity, pellets were frozen in liquid nitrogen and kept at −80 °C till further processing.
steps.searchable_summary: store diatom pellets for chemical extraction

methods.description: Procedure for extracting RNA from diatom samples using TRIZOL and subsequent DNase treatment to remove DNA contamination.

steps.description: RNA extraction was performed following TRIZOL® manufacturer's instructions.
Each sample was treated with DNase I (Invitrogen) using the instruction's manual in order to remove hypothetically contaminating DNA.
steps.searchable_summary: treat RNA samples with DNase I to remove DNA

steps.description: The assessment of RNA quantity was carried out by using Nano-Drop (ND-1000 UV-Vis spectrophotometer; NanoDrop Technologies), monitoring the absorbance at 260 nm and the 260/280 nm and 260/230 nm ratios.
steps.searchable_summary: measure RNA quantity and purity using Nano-Drop
